2605.29744.title: Why Specialist Models Still Matter: A Heterogeneous Multi-Agent Paradigm for Medical Artificial Intelligence
2605.29744.paper_summary: The impressive performance of generalist large language models (LLMs) such as GPT and Claude in healthcare raises a critical question: will domain-specific medical specialist models become obsolete? We argue that the future of medical artificial intelligence (AI) lies not in building monolithic medical foundation models, nor in replacing human expertise, but in orchestrating collaboration among generalist LLMs, domain-specific specialist models, and clinicians. We propose HetMedAgent, a heterogeneous medical multi-agent framework that enables conflict-aware evidence fusion, uncertainty-based clinician intervention triggering, and adaptive threshold calibration. Experiments on three real-world clinical decision-making tasks demonstrate that the synergy between generalist LLMs and domain-specific specialist models significantly outperforms using either type of model alone, validating the irreplaceable value of specialist models in modality-specific analysis. HetMedAgent represents a shift from building medical LLMs or foundation models to multi-agent collaboration, achieving a balance between general reasoning capabilities and domain-specific precision.
2605.29744.paper_summary_zh: 一般大型語言模型（LLMs）如GPT和Claude在醫療領域的卓越表現引發了一個關鍵問題：專門針對某一領域的醫療專家模型會變得過時嗎？我們認為，醫療人工智慧（AI）的未來不在於建造單一的醫療基礎模型，也不在於取代人類專業知識，而在於協調一般LLMs、專門領域模型和臨床醫生之間的合作。我們提出了HetMedAgent，一個異質醫療多代理框架，能夠實現衝突意識的證據融合、基於不確定性的臨床介入觸發和自適應閾值校準。對三個真實世界臨床決策任務的實驗顯示，一般LLMs和專門領域模型之間的協同作用顯著優於單獨使用任何一種類型的模型，驗證了專家模型在特定模態分析中的不可替代價值。HetMedAgent代表了從建造醫療LLMs或基礎模型轉向多代理合作的轉變，實現了一般推理能力與特定領域精確度之間的平衡。
2605.29744.author: Yanan Wang et.al.
2605.29744.authors: Yanan Wang, Shuaicong Hu, Jian Liu, Guohui Zhou, Aiguo Wang, Cuiwei Yang
